en.extract:
The Aceh bulbul (Pycnonotus snouckaerti) is a species of songbird in the bulbul family of passerine birds. It is found in the mountains of north-western Sumatra and is endemic to the island. Its natural habitat is tropical moist lowland forest.


Taxonomy and systematics
The Aceh bulbul was formerly considered as a subspecies of the orange-spotted bulbul until split by the IOC in 2016. Most other authorities have not yet recognized this split.

The '''Aceh bulbul''' ('''''Pycnonotus snouckaerti''''') is a species of [[songbird]] in the [[bulbul]] [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] of [[passerine]] [[bird]]s.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/|title=Bulbuls « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-03-29|archive-date=2017-03-24|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170324112138/http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/|url-status=live}}</ref> It is found in the mountains of north-western [[Sumatra]] and is endemic to the island. Its natural [[habitat]] is tropical moist lowland [[forest]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The Aceh bulbul was formerly considered as a subspecies of the [[orange-spotted bulbul]] until split by the [[International Ornithological Committee|IOC]] in 2016.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/species-updates/|title=Species Updates « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-03-29|archive-date=2021-09-24|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210924131544/https://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/species-updates/|url-status=live}}</ref> Most other authorities have not yet recognized this split.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=B4BD221860077054|title=Pycnonotus snouckaerti - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2017-03-29}}</ref>
